Public moorings are for visiting, not for becoming someone's floating bungalow. Living on a boat while occupying them blocks other river and canal users from stopping, shopping, resting, or visiting. It turns a shared facility into a private berth by stealth.

The iconic 18th century Pulteney Bridge across the River Avon is one of the most recognisable landmarks in Bath and one that millions of tourists flock to the city every year to see. But the lucky owners of houseboats have been able to live right next to the picturesque setting for years. And those who call the area home do not pay to moor at the site as there is 'free navigation' - but some did say they would be willing to pay to stay. However, their days of living rent free along the river could soon be over after the Liberal Democrat-controlled council ordered them out saying they are dangerous, unsafe and branding them a 'hazard'.

  • share Houseboat owners living rent free in beauty spot are ordered to leave

She's not Sporty Spice! Victoria and David Beckham support son Romeo's new clothing range Intra - but there's no getting Posh Spice to model his bomber jacket!

Victoria Beckham and husband David supported their middle son Romeo in his new business venture as he promoted his retro sports brand Intra.  The England footballing legend, 51, and the fashion mogul, 52, looked happier than ever as they posed for Instagram snaps with their beloved sons Romeo, 23, and Cruz, 21, and their girlfriends Kim Turnbull, 25, and Jackie Apostel, 30.  Despite being supporting parents, Posh Spice refused to wear the vintage sport bomber jacket but dad David beamed as he modelled the England themed number alongside Cruz, Jackie and Kim.

By 1943, it was already clear that Adolf Hitler's days were numbered. Defeats on the Eastern Front and in North Africa shifted the momentum of WW2 towards the Allied forces and left the Nazi war machine on the brink of collapse - bringing a premature end to the Fuhrer's desired 'thousand year Third Reich'. Fully aware that the international community would seek retribution and justice for their war crimes, pivotal figures in Hitler's genocidal regime wanted to establish new lives in South America and beyond. Little did they know their secret plans to smuggle out dozens of top-ranking Nazis had been foiled by the forerunner to Britain's MI6, the Secret Intelligence Service.

  • Thoughts & prayers with Cora & all the countless other women whose lives are turned upside down when they realise that their marriages & relationships existed merely to provide a smokescreen of respectability behind which men could hide their sexuality before they has the courage to admit to it. No woman should ever be put through that only to have her life & her entire reality turned upside down, expected to join in with the praise heaped on the man who comes out, her reality ignored & forgotten, reduced to that of a bit part player in the story of the man's brave journey, unable to speak of her own pain because it will be seen as "selfish" or stealing limelight from the "brave" male "main character" of the story. Women's stories have always been placed in the shadows in situations like these. Their pain must be recognised & placed centre stage. I can't imagine what it must be iike for woman like Cora.
  • They are all cowards. That’s not what true sportsmanship is about. He should be able to wear his Arsenal shirt. As I said mobbing him shows very clearly they are not fans. Just ignorant young people
  • Why do I get the feeling that Toney is an accident waiting to happen?
  • Just seen Sky News and one fan said Arsenal were the better team last night and his friend said this team is the best ever in history. What do they smoke in Islington?
  • Why does everyone need to post?. I've worked with people I'm glad to see the back of
  • Bus parade started yesterday on the pitch.
  • We all know the reasons, but we can't mention it.
  • Hard to argue against that
  • Problem is the vast majority of fans have already decided to WFH. 2026 is the WATCH FROM HOME World Cup
  • ".... It’s been an extremely tough couple of years for him and this incident encompasses that.". His last contract with Chelsea was £325,000 a WEEK. There is no excuse for taking drugs and then driving
  • Violence in FIFTEEN cities because a Parisian team won? Something tells me it’s nothing to do with football.
